question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user enumerate all active topic subscriptions by probing the predictable channel name format 'topic.%d' in getTopic(), then selectively inject forged TopicMessages into high-value topic channels to tamper with the Hashgraph history of specific topics?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a TopicMessage with a runningHash that is exactly one byte different from the legitimate hash into the Redis channel, causing subscribe() to propagate a subtly corrupted hash chain that is difficult to detect but invalidates the cryptographic integrity of the Hashgraph topic history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a TopicMessage with a very large message byte array into the Redis channel, causing subscribe() to propagate an oversized message that was never actually submitted to the Hashgraph network, falsifying the content of the topic's message history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a TopicMessage with chunkNum=1 and chunkTotal=1 for a message that was originally multi-chunk in the real Hashgraph history, causing subscribe() to deliver a falsified single-chunk version that misrepresents the original chunked submission?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a TopicMessage with chunkNum exceeding chunkTotal into the Redis channel, causing subscribe() to deliver a structurally invalid chunked message that corrupts the chunk reassembly logic in downstream consumers and misrepresents the Hashgraph topic history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a TopicMessage with a negative or zero sequenceNumber into the Redis channel, and since subscribe() performs no lower-bound validation on sequenceNumber, cause clients to receive a message that appears to precede the first legitimate Hashgraph message, corrupting the sequence history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a TopicMessage with a sequenceNumber gap (e.g., jumping from 5 to 100) into the Redis channel, and since subscribe() does not detect sequence gaps, cause clients to believe messages 6-99 were legitimately skipped in the Hashgraph history, hiding evidence of missing messages?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a TopicMessage with a runningHash of incorrect length (not 48 bytes for SHA-384) into the Redis channel, causing subscribe() to propagate a structurally invalid hash that downstream clients may accept without length validation, corrupting the hash chain integrity of the Hashgraph topic?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a TopicMessage with a null runningHash into the Redis channel, causing subscribe() to deliver a message with no hash to clients, breaking the SHA-384 running hash chain that provides cryptographic proof of Hashgraph message history integrity?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a TopicMessage with a null message byte array into the Redis channel, causing subscribe() to deliver a null-content message that misrepresents the Hashgraph topic history by inserting a phantom message with no actual content?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a TopicMessage with a spoofed topicId matching a high-value or governance topic into the Redis channel for a different topic's channel, and since subscribe() does not cross-validate the deserialized topicId against the channel name, cause clients to receive falsified governance messages that appear to be part of the legitimate Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a TopicMessage with a consensusTimestamp that matches an existing legitimate message's timestamp into the Redis channel, causing subscribe() to deliver a duplicate-timestamp message that creates an ambiguous or conflicting Hashgraph history for clients relying on consensusTimestamp as a unique identifier?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of message authentication codes (MACs) or digital signatures on Redis pub/sub messages consumed by subscribe() to perform a man-in-the-middle attack on the Redis channel, replacing legitimate TopicMessages with forged ones that contain tampered Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a TopicMessage with a runningHashVersion set to a future/unknown version number into the Redis channel, causing subscribe() to propagate a message that signals an unsupported hash version, potentially causing clients to misinterpret or skip hash validation and accept tampered Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a TopicMessage with a validStartTimestamp set before the topic's creation time into the Redis channel, causing subscribe() to deliver a message that appears to predate the topic's existence, falsifying the chronological Hashgraph history of the topic?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a TopicMessage with an initialTransactionId that references a non-existent or different transaction into the Redis channel, causing subscribe() to propagate a message that falsely links the topic submission to a fabricated transaction, corrupting the Hashgraph transaction reference chain?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a TopicMessage with a payerAccountId of a well-known or high-value account into the Redis channel, causing subscribe() to deliver a message that falsely attributes a Hashgraph topic submission to that account, enabling impersonation attacks against the perceived Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a TopicMessage with a sequenceNumber equal to Long.MAX_VALUE into the Redis channel, causing subscribe() to deliver a message that overflows sequence number comparisons in downstream consumers, corrupting the ordering logic of the Hashgraph history stream?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a TopicMessage with a consensusTimestamp of Long.MIN_VALUE or Long.MAX_VALUE into the Redis channel, causing subscribe() to deliver a message with an extreme timestamp that breaks timestamp-based ordering and filtering of the Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a TopicMessage with a message byte array containing binary content that, when interpreted by downstream consumers, triggers deserialization gadget chains, causing subscribe() to act as a vector for delivering malicious payloads disguised as legitimate Hashgraph topic messages?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a high volume of TopicMessages with valid-looking but fabricated sequenceNumbers and runningHashes into the Redis channel, causing subscribe() to overwhelm the onBackpressureBuffer in SharedTopicListener.listen() and trigger BufferOverflowStrategy.ERROR, terminating legitimate subscribers' connections and replacing their history stream with the attacker's injected stream upon reconnection?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a TopicMessage with a runningHash that is the SHA-384 hash of an empty string into the Redis channel, causing subscribe() to propagate a message that resets the running hash chain to a known value, enabling the attacker to predict and forge all subsequent running hashes in the Hashgraph topic history?
